what_it_does: Records and broadcasts a decision signal that the global visual-fidelity decision is decided, selecting the option 'Push the drawn style further first', and states that work gated on that decision may resume.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, board:// address, type=signal, source=claude_code, state=raised, author_session=operator
A single history entry shows state transition from null to raised by operator at 2026-06-22T01:11:56.352023+00:00
Links include an attached_to relationship targeting decision://global/visual-fidelity
Body text names the decision (visual-fidelity), the chosen option, and the gating implication for downstream work
standouts: Decision signal for visual-fidelity is in raised state
Chosen option is 'Push the drawn style further first'
Work gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity is told it can RESUME
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for ledger metadata (id, address, type, state, history, links)
Uses URI schemes board:// and decision://
Follows a signal/noticeboard pattern for publishing decisions
History records who changed state and when
concerns: The phrase 'the resume itself is the lane's/operator's gated step' leaves the actual resumption action and accountability implicit in this file
notes: This noticeboard item broadcasts that the visual-fidelity decision is finalized with the drawn-style-first option and that downstream work gated on that decision can resume.
questions: Which specific lanes or work items are gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity?
What criteria define 'Push the drawn style further first' as complete?
Who performs the actual resume step and how is it recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it records a decided decision and communicates that gated work can resume.
intention_signals: To make a global decision actionable by publishing a machine-readable signal that downstream lanes can consume to resume work under the chosen option.
novelty: Embodies a 'decision.decided signal' pattern where the resume is treated as the lane's/operator's gated step rather than an automatic system transition.
contribution: Contributes a timestamped, attributed decision record with a clear chosen option and gating status that a fused system can use to coordinate downstream work.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard signal item item-49dc1da5 records that the global visual-fidelity decision is decided with the option 'Push the drawn style further first' and that work gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity may resume.
intention_for_embedding: To communicate the chosen visual-fidelity option and authorize downstream gated work to resume.
